GOALS & TRAINING BACKGROUND
MEDICAL, INJURY & PHYSICAL HISTORY
NUTRITION & RELATIONSHIP WITH FOOD
LIFESTYLE SCALES — Rate 1 (poor) to 10 (excellent)
Stress level — how stressed are you on a typical day?
1 = extremely stressed / overwhelmed  ·  10 = completely relaxed
Sleep quality — how well do you sleep?
1 = terrible, broken sleep  ·  10 = deep, 8+ hours, waking refreshed
Energy levels — how is your energy throughout the day?
1 = exhausted, crashes  ·  10 = consistent high energy all day
Motivation to train — how driven are you right now?
1 = forcing yourself  ·  10 = genuinely excited and consistent
Nutrition adherence — how consistent is your eating?
1 = chaotic, no structure  ·  10 = very consistent, intentional

BIOSIGNATURE — 12 SITE CALIPER ASSESSMENT
EPT BioSignature protocol. 12 skinfold sites — each corresponds to a hormonal pattern. Enter mm readings. Ratios calculate automatically.
Required for BF% and lean mass calculation via Durnin-Womersley formula + Siri equation
EPT BioSignature is a pattern-matching framework — not a diagnostic blood test. Site ratios indicate likely hormonal tendencies and direct lifestyle, nutrition and training conversations. Use alongside consultation notes and client history.
01
Cheek
Androgens — Testosterone / DHEA
Directly over the cheekbone, 1cm below the eye socket
High reading relative to triceps: low androgen / testosterone. Common in men with low libido, poor muscle gain, low motivation.
02
Chin
Androgens — Testosterone / DHEA
Directly under the chin, at the midline
Read with cheek. Together they form the androgen profile. High chin relative to triceps reinforces low androgen signal.
03
Pectoral
Androgens / Estrogen (Males)
Halfway between the anterior axillary fold and the nipple, diagonal fold
Males: if pec > triceps — indicates aromatase activity (testosterone converting to estrogen). Often paired with high quad and hamstring readings.
04
Tricep
BASELINE REFERENCE SITE
Posterior midline of arm, midway between acromion and olecranon, vertical fold
Primary reference site. All other sites are compared against this to calculate ratios. This is the benchmark — represents overall lean subcutaneous fat.
05
Subscapular
Insulin — Blood Sugar Management
Just below the inferior angle of the scapula, diagonal fold at 45deg
High relative to triceps: poor carbohydrate tolerance, insulin resistance. Read with supra-iliac — together they form the insulin/carb profile. Address with lower carb nutrition.
06
Mid Axillary
Thyroid — Metabolic Rate
Midaxillary line at the level of the xiphoid process, vertical fold
High relative to triceps: suboptimal thyroid function, low metabolic rate. Often linked to heavy metal or plastics exposure. Fatigue, cold sensitivity, slow recovery common.
07
Supra-iliac (Love Handles)
Insulin — Blood Sugar / Carb Tolerance
Anterior axillary line just above the iliac crest, diagonal fold
The insulin site. Read with subscapular. Both elevated = significant insulin resistance. Primary site for carb tolerance assessment. Responds well to low carb diet and fish oil.
08
Umbilical (Belly Button)
Cortisol — Stress Hormone
2cm directly to the right of the navel, vertical fold
The cortisol site. Can be elevated even in lean individuals. High reading = chronic stress, poor sleep, overtraining. Avoid steady-state cardio — it worsens this site. Prioritise sleep and recovery.
09
Quadriceps
Estrogen — Estrogen Dominance
Anterior thigh, midway between inguinal fold and top of patella, vertical fold
Estrogen site. Read with hamstring. Males: quad should approach triceps value. High quad relative to triceps = elevated estrogen or poor estrogen clearance. Often combined with high pec in males.
10
Hamstring
Estrogen — Estrogen Dominance
Posterior thigh, midway between gluteal fold and popliteal fossa, horizontal fold
Read with quad. Together = estrogen profile. Females: this site has more latitude. High readings in females without elevated quad can indicate progesterone deficiency. Responds to lactate threshold training.
11
Knee
Growth Hormone — Short-term / Sleep Quality
Medial aspect of the knee, just above the patella, horizontal fold
Short-term GH indicator. High reading relative to triceps = recent poor sleep, low GH output. Sensitive to acute sleep deprivation. Read with calf for the full GH picture.
12
Calf
Growth Hormone — Long-term / Sleep Patterns
Medial aspect of calf, at the level of maximal girth, vertical fold
Long-term GH indicator — reflects chronic sleep patterns. High calf relative to triceps = chronically poor sleep quality over weeks/months. Address sleep hygiene, magnesium, blue light reduction.
Saved
HORMONAL PATTERN ANALYSIS — SITE RATIOS vs TRICEP BASELINE
No biosignature assessments saved yet.
EPT INITIAL MOVEMENT SCREEN
Movement quality assessment — not a max strength test. Score each movement, note observations, use findings to direct programming.
3 — Clean
2 — Compensating
1 — Significant Fault
0 — Pain / Skip
01
Front Foot Elevated Split Squat (FFESS)
Unilateral · Lower body · Hip / knee / ankle assessment
Setup: Front foot on 5-10cm elevation (plate or step). Rear foot on floor. Bodyweight only. Hands on hips or crossed on chest.
Execution: 5 controlled reps each side · 3-0-1-0 tempo · Knee tracks over second toe · Front shin angle consistent
What to assess:
  • L vs R depth — does one side drop lower or struggle at a specific point?
  • Knee collapse (valgus) — signals glute med weakness or foot pronation
  • Trunk lean — anterior or lateral — signals hip flexor tightness or core weakness
  • At what rep does form break? (Rep 1 vs Rep 5 = different problem)
  • Heel rise on front foot — ankle dorsiflexion restriction
  • Pain location — anterior knee, lateral hip, groin, ankle
Left Score
Right Score
Observations
02
Goblet Squat
Bilateral · ROM · Proprioception · Depth · General lower body strength
Setup: Feet shoulder-width, toes 15-30deg out. Hold DB or KB at chest (light — 8-12kg). Heels flat on floor first. If restricted, elevate heels on 25mm plate.
Execution: 5 reps · As deep as form allows · Elbows track inside knees at bottom · 3-1-1-0 tempo
What to assess:
  • Depth achieved — above parallel, parallel, below, full? Note if heels elevated
  • Forward trunk lean — hip flexibility vs quad dominance pattern
  • Heel rise without elevation — ankle dorsiflexion restriction (flag for corrective)
  • Knee tracking — collapse inward signals glute/VMO weakness bilaterally
  • Pain — anterior knee (patellar loading), hip impingement, lower back
  • Proprioception — does client understand cues? Can they self-correct?
  • General strength feel — does the weight feel heavy or light?
Score
Weight used (kg)
Depth
Observations
03
Overhead Press — Standing Military
Bilateral · Shoulder flexion · Overhead proprioception · Upper body strength
Setup: Standing. DB or BB. Light-moderate load. Feet hip-width. Brace core. Start at shoulder height.
Execution: 5 reps · Strict — no leg drive · Full lockout overhead · Control the descent · 3-0-1-0 tempo
What to assess:
  • Shoulder flexion range — can they achieve full overhead lockout without arching lower back?
  • Lower back arch — compensates for limited thoracic extension or lat tightness
  • Head forward (chin jut) — upper trap dominance, poor scapular control
  • Elbows flaring — tricep weakness or shoulder instability
  • L vs R asymmetry — one arm lags, one shoulder hitches
  • Pain — anterior shoulder, AC joint, impingement range during lift
  • Proprioception — do they understand where their arms are in space?
Score
Weight used (kg)
Full lockout achieved?
Observations
04
Inverted Row
Bilateral · Pull strength · Scapular control · Tempo listening · Upper back assessment
Setup: Bar at hip height (harder) or higher (easier). Body straight, heels on floor. Overhand grip. Adjust body angle to challenge level.
Execution: Max reps with strict form OR 5 reps at a given tempo · 3-0-1-0 · Pull chest to bar · Full arm extension at bottom
What to assess:
  • Pull strength baseline — how many clean reps before form breaks?
  • Scapular retraction — do they pull with the arms or initiate with the back?
  • Tempo listening — can they control the 3-second eccentric on cue? (Key coaching signal)
  • Hip drop — glute and core engagement under fatigue
  • Elbow flare — rear delt deficit vs lat dominant pattern
  • L vs R shoulder asymmetry — one side leads the pull
Score
Clean reps
Tempo listen
Observations
05
External Rotation — Elbow on Knee
Rotator cuff strength · Shoulder health · Baseline load assessment
Setup: Seated. Elbow resting on knee at 90deg. Forearm vertical. Light DB — trainer selects based on build and apparent strength (start 2-4kg, adjust up).
Execution: 8 reps each side · Forearm rotates outward · 3-1-1-0 tempo · No trunk rotation · Control the return
Trainer load selection guide:
  • Beginner / small frame: start 3kg — increase if moves easily with perfect form
  • Intermediate / medium frame: start 5kg
  • Advanced / large frame: start 8kg — record actual working load
  • Record the load where form is clean for all 8 reps — this is the baseline
  • L vs R difference immediately flags rotator cuff asymmetry
Left Score
Right Score
Left load (kg)
Right load (kg)
Observations
06
Decline Bench Knee Raise
Core strength · Hip flexor vs abdominal differentiation · Lumbopelvic control
Setup: Supine on decline bench (30-45deg). Hands gripping bench behind head. Legs straight at start.
Execution: Max clean reps OR 10 reps assessed · Knees drive to chest · Lower under control · No momentum · Lower back stays flat against bench at all times
What to assess:
  • Lower back peeling off bench — hip flexor dominance, insufficient abdominal strength
  • Momentum vs control — swinging indicates fatigue or avoidance of range
  • Breath holding — assess breathing pattern under load
  • Rep count before form breaks — endurance baseline
  • Asymmetry — one hip hiking or one knee leading consistently
  • Pain — lower back, hip flexor, groin
Score
Clean reps
Observations
07
Hip Hinge Pattern — Romanian Deadlift (DB)
Posterior chain awareness · Hip hinge vs squat pattern · Hamstring flexibility · EPT program cornerstone
Why this is here: The hip hinge is the foundation of nearly every EPT program. Identifying whether a client can hinge (not squat) shapes the entire first mesocycle.
Setup: Feet hip-width. Light DB (8-12kg). Slight knee bend locked in. Hands in front of thighs.
Execution: 5 reps · Push hips back — not down · Bar/DB stays close to legs · Neutral spine · Feel hamstring stretch at bottom · 4-0-1-0 tempo
What to assess:
  • Can they differentiate a hinge from a squat? (Most beginners squat everything)
  • Hamstring flexibility — how far can they reach before rounding?
  • Lower back rounding — flexion under load is a red flag
  • Head position — hyperextension of neck is a compensation pattern
  • Knee bend — locking knees vs excessive squat, find the sweet spot
Score
Weight used (kg)
Pattern understood?
Observations
08
Aerobic Capacity — Row Machine 250m
Optional · Beginner: skip if fatigued · Intermediate/Advanced: aerobic baseline + post-effort response
When to use: Client is moving well after tests 1-7, energy levels good, no significant pain or fatigue signals. Skip if beginner presenting with high fatigue or anxiety.
Execution: 250m for time — maximum effort · Record time · Observe stroke rate, power output, breathing pattern, and critically — recovery after
What to assess:
  • 250m time — baseline for future comparison (note: not standardised, just a marker)
  • Post-effort recovery — how quickly do they return to normal breathing? (<90s = good)
  • Effort willingness — do they push hard or hold back? (Tells you about training mentality)
  • Rowing mechanics — posture, drive sequence, finish position
  • Emotional response post-test — excited, anxious, discouraged? Shapes approach
Time
Recovery <90s?
Observations

PICP STRUCTURAL BALANCE
Poliquin Structural Balance Protocol — Key lifts establish baselines. Auxiliary lifts are assessed as a percentage of the key lift. Deficits below target indicate a weak link requiring remedial C-pairing.
Within PICP range
Minor deficit — monitor
Deficit — prescribe remedial C-pairing
UPPER BODY — Key Lift: Close-Grip Bench Press
All upper body auxiliary lifts are expressed as a percentage of the Close-Grip Bench Press 1RM. Enter your 1RM or best set below — the system will estimate 1RM via Epley formula if reps > 1.
Close-Grip Bench Press KEY LIFT
1RM or best set · Hands shoulder width · Touch chest · Full lockout · Spotter present
Load (kg)Reps
Incline Barbell Press
1RM or best set · 30-45 deg incline · Full ROM · PICP target: 91% of CGBP
Load (kg)Reps
Behind Neck Press (seated)
1RM or best set · Seated · Snatch-width grip · To behind ears · PICP target: 66% of CGBP
Load (kg)Reps
Supinated Chin-Up (total load)
1RM or best set · Supinated grip · Full dead hang · BW + added load · PICP target: 87% of CGBP
Bodyweight (kg)
Added (kg) Reps
Assist (kg) weight removed from BW
Chin-up not yet achieved — flag as deficit. Track assisted reduction over time.
Scott Curl (BB EZ-Bar)
1RM or best set · Strict preacher / Scott bench · Full extension to full contraction · PICP target: 46% of CGBP
Load (kg)Reps
External Rotation — Arm at Side (per side)
8 reps each side · Seated · Elbow at 90deg pinned to side · 3-1-1-0 · No trunk rotation · PICP target: 9.8% of CGBP
Left (kg)Right (kg)
Prone Row 35deg (per hand)
8 reps · Prone on 35deg bench · Elbows to hip · Scapula squeeze · 3-1-1-0 · Enter per hand — system doubles for bilateral total
Per hand (kg)
LOWER BODY — Key Lift: Back Squat
All lower body auxiliary lifts are expressed as a percentage of the Back Squat 1RM. Enter your 1RM or best set below.
Back Squat KEY LIFT
1RM or best set · Below parallel · No knee cave · Heels flat · Controlled descent
Load (kg)Reps
Front Squat
1RM or best set · Clean grip or cross-arm · Upright torso · Below parallel · PICP target: 85% of Back Squat
Load (kg)Reps
Conventional Deadlift
1RM or best set · Full hip extension · Neutral spine · Dead stop · PICP target: 120% of Back Squat
Load (kg)Reps
Lying Leg Curl
8 reps · Full ROM · 4-0-1-0 tempo · Dorsiflexed · No hip lift · Assesses hamstring vs quad balance
Load (kg)

Session Readiness Check-in

Quick pre-session questionnaire. Guides load prescription for the day.

1RM & Load Calculator

Two tools in one: estimate 1RM from any rep/weight set, then calculate the right load for any target rep range.

Step 1 — Estimate 1RM
Enter the weight lifted and reps completed. Uses the Brzycki formula — EPT standard.
Estimated 1RM
—
Brzycki formula: weight × 36 ÷ (37 − reps)
Step 2 — Load by Rep Target
If the estimated 1RM above is set, these calculate automatically. Or enter a 1RM directly.
% 1RM Reference — All Rep Ranges
